description: Skill Content Token Limit Protection - Add max_skill_content_chars parameter to ContextAssembler, truncate oversized skills, add skill summary mode for large skills, add _estimate_total_context_size() warning method, and reporter logging for truncation events.
steps: In kernel/context_assembler.py, add a max_skill_content_chars parameter to __init__ (default 8000 chars per skill)

Modify the _load_skills() method: after calling composer.compose(), check each skill's content length. If a single skill exceeds max_skill_content_chars, truncate to first N chars with '[TRUNCATED - full content in {skill_path}]' appended. For the 'summary mode': if content is oversized, extract only the content before the first ## heading after the intro paragraph.
Add _estimate_total_context_size(sections: list[str]) -> int method that returns total char count and logs a warning via stderr if total exceeds 100000 chars
Wire the estimation into the assemble() method - call it before returning and emit warning if over limit

Add tests in a new file tests/test_context_assembler_truncation.py: test that skills over the limit are truncated, test the summary extraction mode, test the warning is emitted for oversized contexts, test that skills under the limit are NOT truncated
acceptance_criteria: Skills exceeding max_skill_content_chars are truncated with a clear marker
Summary mode extracts only intro+first section for oversized skills
_estimate_total_context_size logs warning to stderr when context > 100K chars
Skills under the limit pass through unmodified
findings: All 820 tests pass with 98.94% coverage. Added 14 new tests for truncation logic. SkillComposer is imported locally in _load_skills() so patch target must be knowledge.skill_composer.SkillComposer not kernel.context_assembler.SkillComposer.
